item.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

item.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

item.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha

item.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

item.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]

item.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

item.title: Apple’s lawsuit couldn’t come at a worse time for OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha, Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

item.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

item.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

item.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.
